content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
October 11, 1989

Michael F. Dean
City Attorney
City of Roseville
311 Vernon Street
Roseville, CA 95678

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-559

Dear Mr. Dean:

This is in response to your letter requesting confirmation of telephone advice I provided to you on September 21, 1989 regarding your responsibilities as City Attorney for Roseville under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ In that telephone conversation you asked whether the solicitation of donations on behalf of the Sunrise Jewish Congregation (the "congregation"), a nonprofit religious organization, from persons in Roseville would create a conflict of interest with respect to your duties as City Attorney in decisions that might affect those persons. You stated that you are also the president and a member of the board of directors of the congregation on a volunteer basis.

As I stated in our telephone conversation, Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or otherwise using his official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. Section 87103 specifies that an official has a financial interest if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on the official or a member of his or her immediate family or on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(b) Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

___________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

File No. A-89-559
Page 2

(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than loans by a commercial lending institution in the regular course of business on terms
available to the public without regard to official
status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars
($250) or more in value provided to, received by or
promised to the public official within 12 months
prior to the time when the decision is made.

(d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

(e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent
for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating two
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.

Section 87103.

A business entity is defined in the Act as "any organization
or enterprise operated for profit, including but not limited to a
proprietorship, partnership, firm, business trust, joint venture,
syndicate, corporation or association." (Section 82005.) Since
the congregation is not an organization or enterprise operated for
profit your involvement with the congregation does not pose a
potential for a conflict of interest under Section 87103(a) or
(d). (Libresco Advice Letter, No. I-88-239, copy enclosed.)

In addition, the fact that you receive no income from the
congregation prevents any potential conflicts of interest with
respect to Section 87103(c). Finally, where persons donate money
to the congregation at your behest, the donations will not be
gifts to you provided you had no discretion in whether to accept
the donation to the congregation. (Section 87103(e); Bremberg
Advice Letter, No. A-89-445, copy enclosed.) Thus, absent some
other disqualifying financial interest as set forth in Section
87103, you may participate in decisions concerning the
congregation.

If any further questions regarding this matter, please feel
free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JWW:plh
Enclosures

FPPC

City of Roseville

Phone 781-0325
311 Vernon Street
Roseville, California
95678

SEP 21 0:07 AM '89

Office of the City Attorney

September 25, 1989

John Wallace
Staff Attorney
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Request for Opinion

Dear Mr. Wallace:

During our conversation on September 21, 1989, you suggested
that my question should be put to you in writing so that you may
confirm your answer and provide an opinion on which I might rely.
This letter therefore asks the Commission for advice pursuant to
the Political Reform Act.

I am the appointed City Attorney of the City of Roseville.
As such, I provide advice to the Council which appoints me, the
City's various commissions, and the staff regarding the various
development projects which come before a City. I am also the
President and a member of the Board of Directors of the Sunrise
Jewish Congregation, a not for profit corporation. I receive no
salary or other remuneration from Sunrise.

Sunrise is in the process of acquiring certain real property
for the purpose of building a sanctuary. The real property is
located outside of the corporate limits of the City of Roseville,
and in a different county. A number of developers who do
business in Roseville, and who share our religious objectives,
have expressed a willingness to make donations to the building
fund of Sunrise, or provide in-kind services.

Please provide your opinion as to whether or not a conflict
of interest will arise should such donations be made or if I
solicit other developers for donations on behalf of Sunrise.

Thank you for your cooperation.

Very truly yours,

MICHAEL F. DEAN
City Attorney

MFD/mlc

sections.question: During our conversation on September 21, 1989, you suggested that my question should be put to you in writing so that you may confirm your answer and provide an opinion on which I might rely. This letter therefore asks the Commission for advice pursuant to the Political Reform Act. I am the appointed City Attorney of the City of Roseville. As such, I provide advice to the Council which appoints me, the City's various commissions, and the staff regarding the various development projects which come before a City. I am also the President and a member of the Board of Directors of the Sunrise Jewish Congregation, a not for profit corporation. I receive no salary or other remuneration from Sunrise. Sunrise is in the process of acquiring certain real property for the purpose of building a sanctuary. The real property is located outside of the corporate limits of the City of Roseville, and in a different county. A number of developers who do business in Roseville, and who share our religious objectives, have expressed a willingness to make donations to the building fund of Sunrise, or provide in-kind services. Please provide your opinion as to whether or not a conflict of interest will arise should such donations be made or if I solicit other developers for donations on behalf of Sunrise.
sections.conclusion: Thus, absent some other disqualifying financial interest as set forth in section 87103, you may participate in decisions concerning the congregation.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a City Attorney have a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act if he solicits donations on behalf of a nonprofit religious congregation from developers who do business in the city?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The City Attorney may solicit donations for the congregation without creating a conflict of interest because the congregation is not a for-profit business entity, the attorney receives no income from it, and donations to the congregation are not gifts to the attorney provided he has no discretion in accepting them.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This document contains both the incoming request (dated September 25, 1989) and the FPPC response letter (dated October 11, 1989). The FPPC response letter is the primary document. Both sections are clearly identifiable. No explicit 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' headers exist, but content is clearly delineated.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: During our conversation on September 21, 1989, you suggested that my question should be put to you in writing so that you may confirm your answer and provide an opinion on which I might rely. This letter therefore asks the Commission for advice pursuant to the Political Reform Act. I am the appointed City Attorney of the City of Roseville. As such, I provide advice to the Council which appoints me, the City's various commissions, and the staff regarding the various development projects which come before a City. I am also the President and a member of the Board of Directors of the Sunrise Jewish Congregation, a not for profit corporation. I receive no salary or other remuneration from Sunrise. Sunrise is in the process of acquiring certain real property for the purpose of building a sanctuary. The real property is located outside of the corporate limits of the City of Roseville, and in a different county. A number of developers who do business in Roseville, and who share our religious objectives, have expressed a willingness to make donations to the building fund of Sunrise, or provide in-kind services. Please provide your opinion as to whether or not a conflict of interest will arise should such donations be made or if I solicit other developers for donations on behalf of Sunrise.

CONCLUSION: Thus, absent some other disqualifying financial interest as set forth in section 87103, you may participate in decisions concerning the congregation.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to City Attorney Michael Dean's request regarding whether his role as president of Sunrise Jewish Congregation creates a conflict of interest when soliciting donations from Roseville developers. The FPPC concludes no conflict exists under the Political Reform Act because the congregation is nonprofit, Dean receives no compensation, and donations are not personal gifts to him.
